title: invalid-signature not a typed error variant
description: When mldsa_verify or ecc_verify returns result \!= 1, the code returns Err(WolfHsmError::Ffi { code: -1, func: 'mldsa_verify: invalid signature' }) (and same for ecc_verify, ed25519_verify). This forces callers who need to distinguish 'signature is cryptographically invalid' from 'transport or protocol error' to match on a synthetic Ffi error and string-compare the func field, which is fragile and undocumented. Suggested rewrite: add WolfHsmError::InvalidSignature to the enum, and return it from all verify functions when the result flag is not 1. The Ffi variant should be reserved for actual FFI call failures.
